title: Gagron Fort
tagline: Gagron Fort — heritage city of Rajasthan

overview.short: Gagron Fort (Hindi/Rajasthani: गागरोन का किला) is a hill and water fort and is situated in Jhalawar district of Rajasthan, in the Hadoti region of India.
overview.description: Gagron Fort (Hindi/Rajasthani: गागरोन का किला) is a hill and water fort and is situated in Jhalawar district of Rajasthan, in the Hadoti region of India. It is located just 5 km from Jhalawar city, the district headquarter. It is an example of a hill and water fort. Raja Madho Bhil laid the foundation of Gagron Fort, after him this fort came under the control of Bijaldev Singh Dod (a Parmar Rajput king) in the twelfth century.

topPlaces.name: Ahu River
topPlaces.description: The Ahu River is an Indian river that starts its journey from the Tonkra hill in Agar tehsil in Agar Malwa district. It flows through the north-western boundary of the district, forming a natural border with Jhalawar District, Rajasthan.

topPlaces.name: Garh Palace, Jhalawar
topPlaces.description: The Garh (or Fort) Palace, Jhalawar, was a royal residence and former administrative headquarters of the rulers of the Jhalawar State in Jhalawar, Rajasthan, India.

topPlaces.name: Jhalawar
topPlaces.description: Jhalawar ([dʒʱaːlaːʋaːɽ] ) is a city, municipal council and headquarter in Jhalawar district of the Indian state of Rajasthan. It is located in the southeastern part of the state.

topPlaces.name: Jhalawar State
topPlaces.description: Jhalawar State was a 15-gun salute (later 13)Princely State in India during the British Raj. It was located in the south eastern historic Hadoti region of Rajputana .The main town in the state was Jhalawar.

topPlaces.name: Jhalrapatan
topPlaces.description: Jhalrapatan is a city and municipality of India in Jhalawar district in the state of Rajasthan. Its population is approximately 37,506.

items.note: The Ahu River is an Indian river that starts its journey from the Tonkra hill in Agar tehsil in Agar Malwa district. It flows through the north-western boundary of the district, forming a natural border with Jhalawar District, Rajasthan.
The Garh (or Fort) Palace, Jhalawar, was a royal residence and former administrative headquarters of the rulers of the Jhalawar State in Jhalawar, Rajasthan, India.
Jhalawar ([dʒʱaːlaːʋaːɽ] ) is a city, municipal council and headquarter in Jhalawar district of the Indian state of Rajasthan. It is located in the southeastern part of the state.

Jhalawar State was a 15-gun salute (later 13)Princely State in India during the British Raj. It was located in the south eastern historic Hadoti region of Rajputana .The main town in the state was Jhalawar.
Jhalrapatan is a city and municipality of India in Jhalawar district in the state of Rajasthan. Its population is approximately 37,506.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Gagron Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Gagron Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Gagron Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kota Airport (68 km) and the nearest railway station is Kota Junction (72 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Gagron Fort?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Gagron Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Gagron Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Gagron Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Gagron Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Ahu River, Garh Palace, Jhalawar, Jhalawar, Jhalawar State, Jhalrapatan.
